mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, which jointly drive corporate earnings growth and produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio over 60-day horizons or fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, which translate into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative alpha in early-cycle regimes or produces a hit rate below 45% over 5-day horizons in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 1.0% while the unemployment rate remains stable, accommodative monetary policy offsets labor market slack, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of observations or shows no statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% confirms sustained economic expansion and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 60% of quarters where GDP growth surpasses the 2.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and retail sales demonstrate synchronized labor market strength and consumer spending, which jointly produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses predictive power with a p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample testing or yields a negative information coefficient.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the consumer price index trigger expectations of tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 70% of months where CPI rises, or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy in walk-forward validation.
